text.title: employee-value-proposition Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for employee-value-proposition as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Generic claims: "innovative culture," "great people," "make a difference."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every employer says this. Generic claims provide zero differentiation and signal that the EVP was written without employee research.

failure-mode.description: One-size-fits-all messaging across all role families.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Engineers care primarily about technical growth; sales reps care about uncapped earnings; ops cares about process and stability. A single message under-converts all three.

failure-mode.description: Never updating the EVP after initial launch.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: EVP becomes stale as benefits, culture, and leadership change. An EVP that no longer matches reality is worse than no EVP — it creates disappointed hires who become Glassdoor detractors.

failure-mode.description: Direct translation of a US-centric EVP to EU/LATAM markets without localization (e.g., equity framing in countries where options are taxed at grant).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Localization failures produce legally inaccurate or culturally tone-deaf offers that candidates reject at higher rates than generic ones.
